intent1: Implement the ML evaluation report engine defined in `/app/docs/spec_v1.md`.

rounds.title: Implement the ML evaluation report engine defined in `/app/docs/spec_v1.md`.
rounds.md:
Implement the ML evaluation report engine defined in `/app/docs/spec_v1.md`.

The spec defines a CLI tool called `mlreport` with two subcommands: `evaluate` (compute metrics from raw predictions and generate reports) and `compare` (compare two model evaluations and detect regressions). The tool must handle all three task types (binary classification, multiclass classification, regression), produce both Markdown and JSON reports, and enforce compound regression rules during comparison. All computation rules, formatting, and CLI semantics are in the spec.

rounds.title: The specification has been updated to v2 (`/app/docs/spec_v2.md`). The changes from v1 inc
rounds.md:
The specification has been updated to v2 (`/app/docs/spec_v2.md`). The changes from v1 include:

1. **Bootstrap Confidence Intervals**: The `evaluate` command now supports bootstrap confidence interval computation for metrics. New flags `--bootstrap`, `--confidence`, and `--seed` control this feature. Confidence intervals should appear in both JSON and Markdown report outputs.

2. **sMAPE replaces MAPE**: The regression metric previously named `mape` has been corrected to `smape` (Symmetric Mean Absolute Percentage Error) with an updated formula. The metric key changes from `mape` to `smape` in all outputs.

Update the implementation to match the new specification. All existing functionality from v1 must continue to work (with the MAPE-to-sMAPE correction applied).

rounds.title: The specification has been updated to v3 (`/app/docs/spec_v3.md`). The changes from v2 inc
rounds.md:
The specification has been updated to v3 (`/app/docs/spec_v3.md`). The changes from v2 include:

1. **Metric Direction-Aware Regression Detection (conflict with v2)**: The `compare` command's regression detection now distinguishes between "higher-is-better" metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, f1, auc_roc, r_squared, etc.) and "lower-is-better" metrics (mse, rmse, mae, log_loss, smape). Previously, all metrics used the same delta direction for regression checks (`delta < min_absolute_delta`). Now, higher-is-better metrics regress when they decrease, and lower-is-better metrics regress when they increase. The regression config format changes from `min_absolute_delta`/`min_relative_delta` to `max_delta`/`max_relative_delta` (non-negative values representing maximum allowed worsening). Old config field names must still be accepted for backward compatibility. The regression analysis output now includes a "direction" field.

2. **Split Filtering (extension)**: The `evaluate` command now accepts a `--split` flag to filter evaluation to specific splits. Comma-separated split names are supported. Only specified splits appear in the output.

Update the implementation to match the new specification. All existing functionality from v1/v2 must continue to work (with the regression detection direction change applied). Old-format regression configs using `min_absolute_delta`/`min_relative_delta` must still be accepted.

rounds.title: The specification has been updated to v4 (`/app/docs/spec_v4.md`). The changes from v3 inc
rounds.md:
The specification has been updated to v4 (`/app/docs/spec_v4.md`). The changes from v3 include:

1. **Summary Subcommand (extension)**: A new `summary` subcommand has been added. It reads an existing `report.json` (generated by the `evaluate` command) and outputs a concise single-line summary to stdout. The summary includes the model name, version, task type, number of splits, and primary metrics (averaged across splits). Primary metrics vary by task type: `accuracy`, `f1`, `auc_roc` for binary; `accuracy`, `f1_macro` for multiclass; `rmse`, `mae`, `r_squared` for regression. Summary metric values are rounded to 4 decimal places.

Update the implementation to add the new `summary` subcommand. All existing functionality from v1/v2/v3 must continue to work unchanged.

rounds.title: The specification has been updated to v5 (`/app/docs/spec_v5.md`). The changes from v4 inc
rounds.md:
The specification has been updated to v5 (`/app/docs/spec_v5.md`). The changes from v4 include:

1. **Bootstrap CI correction for regression tasks**: In previous versions, the `smape` metric was excluded from bootstrap confidence interval computation for regression tasks. This has been corrected. The regression CI metrics are now: `mse`, `rmse`, `mae`, `r_squared`, `smape`. When bootstrap is enabled for a regression evaluation, `smape` must now have a confidence interval computed and included in the output.

2. **`--exclude-metric` flag for `compare` command (extension)**: A new optional `--exclude-metric` flag has been added to the `compare` command. It accepts a comma-separated list of metric names to exclude from regression analysis. Excluded metrics still appear in the performance delta table (both Markdown and JSON), but they are not checked for regression and do not appear in the regression analysis table or JSON output. If a metric name in the exclusion list does not exist in the evaluation results or regression config, it is silently ignored.

Update the implementation to support both changes. All existing functionality from v1/v2/v3/v4 must continue to work unchanged.

rounds.title: The specification has been updated to v6 (`/app/docs/spec_v6.md`). The changes from v5 inc
rounds.md:
The specification has been updated to v6 (`/app/docs/spec_v6.md`). The changes from v5 include:

1. **Bootstrap default change (conflict with v2-v5)**: The `--bootstrap` flag for the `evaluate` command now defaults to `0` instead of `1000`. This means confidence intervals are no longer computed by default. To enable CI computation, you must explicitly pass `--bootstrap N` where `N > 0`. This is a breaking change from v2-v5 where CIs were computed by default with 1000 iterations.

2. **`--split` flag for `compare` command (extension)**: A new `--split` flag has been added to the `compare` command. It accepts a comma-separated list of split names. When specified, only those splits are compared, and the validation is relaxed: only the specified splits need to exist in both baseline and candidate manifests (other splits can differ). When `--split` is not specified, the existing behavior is preserved: all splits must match exactly. If a specified split does not exist in either baseline or candidate, the tool exits with code 2.

Update the implementation to match the new specification. All existing functionality from v1/v2/v3/v4/v5 must continue to work unchanged (except where explicitly overridden by the conflict).

rounds.title: The specification has been updated to v7 (`/app/docs/spec_v7.md`). The changes from v6 inc
rounds.md:
The specification has been updated to v7 (`/app/docs/spec_v7.md`). The changes from v6 include:

1. **Regression analysis metric ordering (correction)**: The compare command's regression analysis table in Markdown output previously listed metrics in alphabetical order (using `sort.Strings`). This was an oversight from v3. The table must now list metrics in the standard metric order for the task type, matching the order used in the evaluate output. For regression: `mse`, `rmse`, `mae`, `r_squared`, `smape`. For binary classification: `accuracy`, `precision`, `recall`, `f1`, `auc_roc`, `log_loss`. Only metrics present in the regression analysis are listed, but they follow the standard order. This change only affects Markdown output; JSON uses unordered object keys.

2. **`--json` flag for `summary` command (extension)**: A new `--json` flag has been added to the `summary` command. When `--json` is present, the command outputs a JSON object to stdout instead of the text summary line. The JSON structure includes `model` (with `name`, `version`, `task_type`), `split_count` (integer), and `primary_metrics` (object mapping metric names to float values rounded to 4 decimal places). When `--json` is not present, behavior is unchanged (text line to stdout).

Update the implementation to match the new specification. All existing functionality from v1-v6 must continue to work unchanged.
